seo.title: Building Code Generation with n8n AI Agents + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build code generation with n8n AI Agents + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: n8n AI Agents enable powerful code generation workflows where LLMs produce, test, and refine code iteratively within sandboxed environments. However, these agentic chains introduce high risks like malicious code injection, credential leaks in generated scripts, and infinite recursion from unvalidated outputs. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify code safety, syntax, and execution integrity before deployment.

architectureOverview.heading: Code Generation Architecture with n8n AI Agents
architectureOverview.description:
Typical n8n code generation workflows start with a trigger (webhook/HTTP request) feeding user prompts to an AI Agent node configured with tools for code generation, sandbox execution, and testing. The agent uses a language model (e.g., OpenAI/Groq) to generate code, which passes to a Code Sandbox node for execution and a validation node for test assertions. Iterative refinement loops back through memory nodes if tests fail, with final approved code output to deployment nodes like GitHub or serverless functions.

Memory window nodes maintain conversation context across iterations, while credential nodes (e.g., API keys) are injected securely. Failure modes emerge when unvalidated AI outputs trigger recursive loops or expose secrets in tool parameters. Vex integrates as a webhook node post-AI Agent to intercept and verify outputs before sandbox execution.

This architecture excels for rapid prototyping but demands rigorous safety checks given the high-risk nature of executing AI-generated code.

risks.name: Recursive Agent Execution
risks.description: Workflow trigger chains cause infinite loops when AI nodes output triggers that re-execute the workflow without termination conditions.
risks.mitigation: Insert Vex webhook to validate output structure and detect recursion patterns before passing to next nodes.

risks.name: Unvalidated Code Output
risks.description: AI-generated code bypasses syntax/safety checks before sandbox execution, risking malicious payloads or runtime errors.
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies syntax validity, scans for dangerous patterns (eval, fs.write), and confirms test coverage.

risks.name: Credential Leakage
risks.description: Generated code or tool parameters inadvertently expose API keys/credentials from n8n credential nodes.
risks.mitigation: Vex redacts and scans for credential patterns in code strings and execution traces.

risks.name: Stale Memory Context
risks.description: Memory window nodes inject outdated conversation history, leading to context drift and flawed code iterations.
risks.mitigation: Vex validates memory payloads for recency and relevance before agent re-processing.

implementation.title: n8n AI Agent Node for Code Generation
implementation.description: Configure the AI Agent node with a code generation tool that produces executable TypeScript/Python.

implementation.title: Vex Webhook Node Setup
implementation.description: Add Vex webhook after AI Agent to intercept code output for verification.

implementation.title: Code Sandbox Node with Vex Approval
implementation.description: Only execute code after Vex verification passes all checks.

implementation.title: Test Validation Loop
implementation.description: IFTTT-style loop that feeds test failures back to AI Agent via memory node.

vexIntegration.title: Install Vex SDK in n8n
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex verification using TypeScript SDK in a Code node before sandbox execution.

vexIntegration.title: Real-time Code Safety Check
vexIntegration.description: Verify generated code blocks malicious patterns and secret exposure.

vexIntegration.title: Sandbox Execution Guardrail
vexIntegration.description: Pre-validate sandbox inputs and monitor execution traces.
